input_args.parameters_json_dump: '{"abstract": "UNLABELLED_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\nPREMISE OF THE STUDY_x000D_\\nTreelines are temperature-sensitive ecotones that should be able to expand in response to global warming; however, they are also controlled by ecological constraints. These constraints can create bottlenecks for tree regeneration, hindering treeline advances. Near Kangiqsualujjuaq (Nunavik, subarctic Qu\\u00c3\\u00a9bec), previous studies suggested successful recruitment of Larix laricina above the altitudinal treeline, while Picea mariana establishment remains scarce. We studied regeneration of both species to identify factors responsible for such contrasting responses. \\u00e2\\u20ac\\u00a2_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\nMETHODS_x000D_\\nWe measured seeds and wings to evaluate species dispersal potential. We compared seed viability and tolerance to shrub leachates with germination trials. To evaluate seedbed preferences, we compared seedling occurrence on the different seedbeds with seedbed relative abundance in the field. \\u00e2\\u20ac\\u00a2_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\nKEY RESULTS_x000D_\\nSeed germination was similar between L. laricina and P. mariana, whereas dispersal potential was higher for the latter. Germination of P. mariana seeds was more strongly inhibited by shrub leachates than were L. laricina seeds. In the field, we found only a few Picea seedlings, but numerous seedlings of Larix had established disproportionally on several seedbeds. While Betula glandulosa, mosses, and Vaccinium uliginosim impeded Larix establishment, numerous seedlings were found on lichens, mineral soil, and liverworts. The low occurrence of suitable seedbeds for Picea, mainly mineral soil, could explain the seedling scarcity of this species. \\u00e2\\u20ac\\u00a2_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\n_x000D_\\nCONCLUSIONS_x000D_\\nThis study highlighted that allelopathy and unsuitable seedbeds could contribute to regeneration failure of P. mariana in eastern Nunavik and emphasizes the need to consider ecological preferences of species before predicting treeline expansion under a warmer climate.", "max_output_tokens": 4096, "model": "gpt-5-mini", "reasoning": {"effort": "low"}, "system_message": "You are EcodataGPT, a structured data extraction engine.\\n\\nGoal: extract fields from the user\'s abstract into the provided schema.\\n\\nRules:\\n- Only use information explicitly supported by the text.
